current.summary: Designed TUI confirm-bucket feature (6 panel reviews, 7 blocking + 23 important resolved), wrote implementation plan (10 phases, 30 tasks), created 48 beads with dependency chains, completed Phase B analysis, started sequential execution — 2 of 48 beads done (1.1a RED + 1.1b GREEN: ReadFileContent path containment).

activeWork.description: Sequential execution of 48 beads for TUI confirm-bucket rendering (triage step + metadata tier badges)
activeWork.progress: 2/48 beads complete. Phase 1 (Security): Task 1.1 done (ReadFileContent path containment — test + fix). Task 1.2 (SafeResolve utility) is next ready bead. Also ready in parallel: 2.1a (Meta fields) and 3.1a (TierForMeta) — but executing sequentially per user choice.

git.lastCommit: 78591d4 docs: TUI confirm-bucket implementation plan — 10 phases, 30 tasks

recentDecisions: 6 panel reviews with 4 personas each — 7 blocking + 23 important issues found and resolved
Renamed 'Confirm' step to 'Triage' (semantic collision with Review)
No triage drill-in — preview pane is sufficient (avoids state collision with review drill-in)
Disable content-signal fallback for provider sources (pattern detection sufficient for provider dirs)
enter = advance (not toggle/drill-in), space = sole toggle key in triage step
All analyzer metadata (confidence, detection_source, detection_method) stripped from incoming YAML via allowlist
Sequential execution mode chosen for 48 beads

Phase B found 8 risks: renderButton undefined, viewTriage stub gap, mergeConfirmIntoDiscovery forward dep, test bug in 9.4, triage-preview zone mark missing, nil pointer in merge, analyzerToast retroactive signature change, copySupportingFiles already skips .syllago.yaml
nextSteps: Continue sequential bead execution: next is syllago-60z9l (1.2a: RED — SafeResolve symlink escape test)
Then syllago-q9fpw (2.1a: RED — Meta confidence fields roundtrip test)
Then syllago-klllx (3.1a: RED — TierForMeta test cases)
Commit the 2 uncommitted files (fileinfo.go + fileinfo_test.go) before continuing
Use /develop --resume or /develop tui-confirm-bucket to continue the workflow

context.notes: The .develop/tui-confirm-bucket.json state file tracks the full workflow state (stage=execute, beads.created=true). Phase B analysis at .develop/tui-confirm-bucket-phase-b.md documents 8 risks for implementing agents — particularly renderButton being undefined in the TUI codebase (Task 6.1 uses it but it doesn't exist). The plan was quality-reviewed and validated with 2 passes (3 gaps found and fixed on first pass). The design doc went through 6 panel reviews with zero blocking issues on panels 5 and 6. First RED test caught a real issue: test initially passed for wrong reason (file not found vs path traversal), had to restructure fixture to make traversal paths reach actual files.
